steps.title: Spot the verb tense pattern
steps.content: The sentence uses present tense for “there is a tortoise” and “appears in both of the play's parallel timelines,” establishing how the tortoise's role is described.

steps.title: Apply verb tense consistency
steps.content: The verb tense consistency rule requires all verbs in a sentence to use the same tense unless there's a clear reason for a shift. Goes in “goes by two names” (meaning “is known as”) matches the verb tense of “appears.” The other three options introduce past or future tenses without justification. That matches choice A — A is correct.
